mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate transmits through money-market pricing and term premium repricing to push the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next month. When FEDFUNDS is already elevated, any persistence in that level keeps real-rate expectations and front-end yield pressure firm, so the 10-year yield rises rather than falls.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS readings are followed by 20-trading-day declines in the 10-year yield in more than 60% of observed windows, or if the sign relation flips during the same tightening plateau regime.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the recent monthly pace, inflation expectations reprice upward and the 10-year Treasury yield rises within the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest after a clear inflation surprise, because nominal duration is most sensitive when the market revises the expected path of future prices.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration is not followed by a positive 20-day change in the 10-year yield at least 55% of the time, or if median yield change after triggers is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future demand, which pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next month. The mechanism is strongest when payroll gains stall while unemployment is rising, because growth-sensitive inflation expectations and policy-rate expectations both soften.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-softening trigger is followed by higher 20-day 10-year yields in more than 40% of cases, or if yield changes are indistinguishable from zero after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates firmer consumer spending and demand resilience, which raises expected nominal growth and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the following month. The signal fails when retail sales growth is driven by temporary price effects without confirming strength in other demand proxies.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS strength does not precede positive 20-day changes in the 10-year yield in at least 55% of rolling samples, or if the estimated effect is negative in consumer-expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, the economy is absorbing more factory output without a matching housing-capacity response, implying firmer real activity and higher nominal rate pressure. In that configuration the 10-year Treasury yield should rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and HOUST stays below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint production-and-housing condition is followed by falling 10-year yields in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if INDPRO alone explains the effect without HOUST confirming it.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 growth after prior expansion is a direct signal that aggregate demand is slowing, which lowers expected inflation and reduces the 10-year Treasury yield over the next quarter. This effect should be visible only after the quarterly GDP print confirms a downshift from the earlier trend.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP downshifts are not followed by lower 60-trading-day 10-year yields in at least 55% of cases, or if the yield response is positive in growth-deceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS improves while UNRATE does not fall, the labor market is adding jobs but not tightening enough to raise inflation pressure, so the 10-year Treasury yield should drift lower as the market interprets the data as growth without wage acceleration. The signal is most informative when payroll gains are modest rather than explosive.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month over month while UNRATE is flat or higher
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE pattern produces higher 5-day 10-year yields more than 60% of the time, or if the pattern has no measurable directional effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate combined with firm CPIAUCSL keeps real-rate expectations and term premium pressure upward, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next month. The mechanism fails if inflation is cooling rapidly enough to offset the policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation condition is followed by lower 20-day 10-year yields in more than 50% of cases, or if the effect disappears once inflation cools below its 6-month average.
